summary: PATH-poisoning shim shadows `node` to achieve install-time RCE under Bun
details: The package declares `"bin": { "node": "./shim.js" }` in package.json, which causes the package manager to place an attacker-controlled `node` executable into `node_modules/.bin/`. It also declares `protoc-gen-grpc-web` as a dependency — a Bun-default-trusted package whose postinstall script invokes `node`. When a developer runs `bun i` on this package, the trusted dependency's postinstall resolves `node` from `node_modules/.bin/` first and executes `shim.js` instead of the real Node.js interpreter, achieving arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine at install time. The shipped shim.js writes a marker file to `/tmp/.bun-npm-pwned` and prints a "PATH POISONED" banner, confirming the attack primitive works; the `bin` name `node` is not a legitimate CLI this package provides — its only purpose is to shadow the system interpreter. This is a working install-time arbitrary-code-execution vector against any developer using Bun to install this package directly or transitively.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
